title: Bridge Paper: The psychedelic renaissance: the next trip for psychiatry?
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nonordinary Rendering** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Psychedelics — DMN disruption, ego dissolution, altered states, predictive processing decoupling.
**Phase context:** psychedelic, psilocybin, DMT, ego dissolution, ayahuasca, altered states, default mode network

Paper: The psychedelic renaissance: the next trip for psychiatry?
DOI: 10.1017/ipm.2019.39
Abstract: The psychedelic research renaissance is gaining traction. Preliminary clinical studies of the hallucinogenic fungi, psilocybin, with psychological support, have indicated improvements in mood, anxiety and quality of life. A seminal, open-label study demonstrated marked reductions in depression symptoms in participants with treatment-resistant depression (TRD).
